mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based private demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in over 55 percent of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising industrial production confirms resilient manufacturing output that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO signals during tightening periods produce negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands corporate revenue capacity and consumer spending power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4 percent, refuting the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal consumption expenditures grow faster than real GDP, private sector demand outpaces aggregate output, fueling positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The consumption-GDP growth spread fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, declining industrial production signals capacity constraints and slowing manufacturing activity, reducing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO signals during late-cycle phases yield positive 20-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of cases, invalidating the earnings downgrade channel.
